task_desc: Implement Claude-compatible tool-result persistence + transcript placeholders: persist tool_result contents under `projects/<projectKey>/<sessionId>/tool-results/` as `.txt` or `.json`, and write `<persisted-output>...Use Read to view</persisted-output>` placeholders (plus `[Old tool result content cleared]` marker where used) for compaction/compat surfaces; ensure Read allowlists cover these files.
task_context: Why: Claude Code includes an evidence-backed tool-result offload mechanism and placeholder markers (`<persisted-output>...</persisted-output>` and `[Old tool result content cleared]`). Kode currently has bash-output persistence (`bash-outputs/`) but lacks Claude-compatible `tool-results/` + placeholder surfaces, which impacts transcript compaction, resume stability, and log size.
Official evidence anchors:
- `docs/research/claude-code/07_runtime_specs.md` R4.1/R4.2 (tool-results location + markers + rewrite behavior)
- Official `cli.js` needles indexed in `docs/research/claude-code/07_runtime_specs.md` (DX0="tool-results", pG1, KX0, VX0)
Kode integration points:
- `packages/protocol/src/utils/kodeAgentSessionLog.ts` (session persistence layer; best place to keep `.jsonl` compact and compatible)
- `packages/core/src/permissions/fileToolPermissionEngine/plan.ts` (special allowed read reasons for tool-results under current session)
Constraints:
- No guessing: implement only what we can justify from the official evidence; mark any unknown thresholds/selection rules as unknown in docs.
- Kode-first: persist under `.kode` root (via `getKodeRoot()`); legacy `.claude` remains read-compat/import only.
- Avoid data loss: if persistence fails, do not silently drop tool output from logs.

Will also update file read allowlists in `packages/core/src/permissions/fileToolPermissionEngine/plan.ts` so the Read tool can open these persisted result files without prompting.

Implementation ready for review:

- Evidence-backed pivot: Verified official Claude Code implements tool-result offload via `P65` + `m9A` with default max size `_92=400000` and preview `j92=2000` (see `cli.js:1716`), producing a `<persisted-output>...Output too large...Full output saved to...Preview...` placeholder (via `T65`).

- Implemented the same oversized-tool-result behavior in KodeŌĆÖs tool pipeline (so it affects model context + session logs):
  - Added `packages/core/src/utils/toolResultPersistence.ts` with a Claude-compatible placeholder format and file persistence to `projects/<projectKey>/<sessionId>/tool-results/<tool_use_id>.(txt|json)`.

task_desc: Fix async tool description handling end-to-end: ensure tool descriptions are awaited/cached before any adapter builds tool schemas (so non-Anthropic adapters donŌĆÖt fall back to `Tool: <name>`), and ensure split/adapters never leak an async `description` function where a string is expected. task_context: Official evidence (2.1.6): `/Users/baicai/Desktop/MyT/Kode/KodeOrch/other/1/claude-code/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code/cli.js:4954` shows tool schema building uses awaited values (e.g. `description:await W.prompt(...)`).
Kode current state:
- Tool interface allows `description?: string | ((input?)=>Promise<string>)` and provides `resolveToolDescription()` + `cachedDescription` (good).

- However, `splitLegacyTool()` currently passes through `tool.description` without awaiting, and non-Anthropic adapters (OpenAI) use `getToolDescription()` which returns a fallback if `cachedDescription` is unset. There is no global pre-resolution step for CLI sessions.
Acceptance criteria:
- Before any request that needs string descriptions, all enabled tools have `cachedDescription` populated via `resolveToolDescription(tool)`.
- `splitLegacyTool().spec.description` is never an async function; it is either a string or undefined, with `cachedDescription` used as the primary source.
- Unit tests cover: async description tool becomes correct string in the adapter schema path; no regressions for MCP server listing.

task_desc: Implement Claude-like automatic skills hot-reload: when skills/commands files under `.kode/skills`, `.claude/skills`, `~/.kode/skills`, or `~/.claude/skills` change, the running session should pick them up without restart (by auto-calling `reloadCustomCommands()` and refreshing any cached listing). task_context: Official requirement (CHANGELOG): `/Users/baicai/Desktop/MyT/Kode/KodeOrch/other/1/claude-code/CHANGELOG.md` (v2.1.0) includes: "Added automatic skill hot-reload - skills created or modified in ~/.claude/skills or .claude/skills are now immediately available without restarting the session".
Kode current state:

- Skills/slash commands are loaded through `apps/cli/src/services/customCommands/loader.ts` via a memoized `loadCustomCommands()`; changes are only picked up after manual `reloadCustomCommands()` (e.g. /refresh).
- No long-lived watcher currently auto-triggers reloads for skill/command file changes.
Constraints:
- No heavy chokidar dependency unless absolutely needed; prefer `fs.watch` + watching SKILL.md files and command markdown files, plus parent dir rename events for new skills.
- Must not spam reload: debounce changes and avoid blocking the UI thread.
Acceptance criteria:
- Editing an existing `SKILL.md` or adding a new skill directory makes it appear in SkillTool prompt without restarting the session and without needing a manual refresh command.
- Unit tests validate reload is triggered on simulated file changes (test helper hooks if needed).

- Added a debounced custom-command watcher subsystem:
  - `apps/cli/src/services/customCommands/events.ts`: subscribe/emit reload events.
  - `apps/cli/src/services/customCommands/reload.ts`: `reloadCustomCommandsForSession()` clears loader caches and emits a reload event.
  - `apps/cli/src/services/customCommands/watcher.ts`: `startCustomCommandWatcher()` uses `fs.watch` on discovered skill/command roots, debounces changes, and rebuilds watchers after reload so newly-created dirs/files are picked up.

  - `apps/cli/src/entrypoints/cli/setup.ts`: starts the watcher (skips in tests via existing `NODE_ENV === 'test'` early return).
  - `apps/cli/src/ui/screens/REPL/useReplController.tsx`: keeps `commands` in React state and subscribes to reload events; on reload it re-runs `getCommands()` and shows a toast so users know commands refreshed.

  - `apps/cli/src/commands/builtin/refreshCommands.ts`
  - `apps/cli/src/services/pluginRuntime.ts`
  - `apps/cli/src/commands/plugin/plugin.ts`
- Key design choice: avoid relying on `fs.watch({ recursive: true })` because it is not reliable across Bun/Node/platforms; instead watch the specific discovery roots and re-scan/rebuild watchers on reload.
